original_image_tags: A single page of a handwritten letter in blue ink on off-white paper. The letter is a continuation of a previous page. Two hole-punch marks are visible at the top edge.

elements.description: A scanned page of handwritten cursive text on stationery with a printed letterhead reading 'Chimney Farm' and 'NOBLEBORO, MAINE' at the top left. The handwritten text describes an observation of a 'flying saucer' and ends with a signature 'Elizabeth C. Beston' (with a crossed-out word in blue ink) and a typed/bracketed '(Mrs. Henry Beston)' below it. A handwritten number '3' in red ink is visible in the bottom left margin.

judge.reason: Description B claims the presence of a printed letterhead, specific handwritten text about a 'flying saucer', a specific signature, and a red handwritten number, none of which are mentioned or contradicted by the much vaguer Description A, creating a major discrepancy in the claimed visual content.
